attacking_style: Launches multiple explosive accelerations in final 2-4km of steep climbs, testing rivals before decisive move. Complements uphill aggression with fearless, technical descending to solo clear or regain contact.
team_tactics: Teams place him in breakaways when protected leader exists (Yates), enabling low-pressure stage hunting. Limited domestique support on GC attempts; often isolated on final climbs when co-leadership collapses.
terrain_preference: Excels on brutal, short-steep gradients (Etna-type) where explosive accelerations matter. Strong technical descender. Struggles on sustained 8%+ climbs and rolling power terrain where Sky/INEOS tempo grinds.
vulnerability: Catastrophic form collapses mid-Grand Tour (lost 4+ minutes, 10+ minutes in separate Vuelta/Giro editions). Mechanicals prove disastrous due to lack of team protection. Weakens when isolated early on long climbs.
key_patterns: Multiple accelerations before decisive attack (3+ surges in final 3km documented repeatedly)
Uses descending aggression tactically: regains contact after being distanced, or extends solo advantages
Mid-race form collapses recurring pattern: 2017 Vuelta, 2018 Giro both show sudden 4-10min losses
Strongest when attacking protected trains (INEOS 2021) rather than defending GC with weak team support
Career resurgence evident 2021-2023 after 2019 struggles, climbing form returning to stage-winning level
form_trajectory: positive: 2021 Catalunya win ended two-year drought, 2022 Dauphiné top-10, 2023 Catalunya near-solo suggests climbing resurgence after career struggles 2017-2020
